reason: The passage describes the exodus from Egypt where God instructs Moses and Aaron to lead the people out. While the text mentions that Egypt urged the people to leave hastily ('וַתֶּחֱזַק מִצְרַיִם עַל־ הָעָם לְמַהֵר לְשַׁלְּחָם' - 'Egypt grew strong upon the people to hasten sending them away'), this urgency comes from the Egyptians, not from Hebrew leaders. Moses and Aaron are portrayed as obediently carrying out God's commands, with the people complying and following instructions. There is no depiction of Hebrew leaders raging against the people; rather, the text shows orderly departure with the people preparing their belongings and following the prescribed Passover ceremony.
